checklist.property: deposit() is callable by any external address without whitelist or owner check, violating the requirement that only whitelisted addresses or the owner may call deposit(). Verify that a require(whitelist[msg.sender] || msg.sender == owner) guard or equivalent modifier is present.
checklist.threat_model_filter: external_caller -> deposit() is unrestricted, violating whitelist_or_owner boundary

checklist.property: emergencyUnpause() at line 45 is missing the onlyOwner modifier, allowing any external address to unpause the contract and re-enable deposits and withdrawals. Verify that onlyOwner modifier is applied to emergencyUnpause().
checklist.threat_model_filter: external_caller -> emergencyUnpause() is unrestricted, violating owner_only boundary

checklist.property: withdraw() uses low-level .call{value: amount}('') at line 35. Verify that the boolean return value is checked with require(success) to prevent silent transfer failures.
checklist.threat_model_filter: assumption: External calls via .call{} may trigger reentrant execution

checklist.property: withdraw() makes external call via .call{value: amount} at line 35 without updating any state variables (balances mapping is never decremented). This violates the checks-effects-interactions pattern and creates a reentrancy vector where the recipient can re-enter before state reflects the withdrawal.
checklist.threat_model_filter: assumption: External calls via .call{} may trigger reentrant execution

checklist.property: withdraw(amount) succeeds and transfers ETH when msg.sender == owner, paused == false, and amount <= address(this).balance.
withdraw(amount) reverts with 'Not owner' when msg.sender != owner.
checklist.threat_model_filter: external_caller -> withdraw() must reject unauthorized callers

checklist.property: addToWhitelist(addr) sets whitelist[addr] = true when msg.sender == owner, and removeFromWhitelist(addr) sets whitelist[addr] = false when msg.sender == owner.
addToWhitelist(addr) and removeFromWhitelist(addr) revert with 'Not owner' when msg.sender != owner.
checklist.threat_model_filter: external_caller -> whitelist management must reject unauthorized callers

checklist.property: deposit() succeeds when msg.sender is in the whitelist mapping and msg.value > 0.
deposit() reverts when msg.sender is neither whitelisted nor owner, regardless of msg.value.
checklist.threat_model_filter: external_caller -> deposit() must reject unauthorized callers

checklist.property: emergencyPause() reverts when msg.sender != owner, and emergencyUnpause() reverts when msg.sender != owner.
checklist.threat_model_filter: external_caller -> pause/unpause must reject unauthorized callers

checklist.property: deposit() increases balances[msg.sender] by exactly msg.value. Verify balances[msg.sender] after deposit equals balances[msg.sender] before deposit + msg.value.
deposit() correctly handles multiple sequential deposits from the same address, accumulating balances without loss or overflow (protected by Solidity ^0.8.20).
deposit() emits Deposited(msg.sender, msg.value) with correct indexed sender address and exact amount on every successful deposit.
withdraw(amount) emits Withdrawn(msg.sender, amount) with correct indexed recipient address and exact amount on every successful withdrawal.
After emergencyPause() is called by the owner, both deposit() and withdraw() revert with 'Paused' for all callers.
emergencyPause() is idempotent — calling it when already paused succeeds without adverse effects or unexpected state changes.

withdraw() uses amount <= address(this).balance comparison, correctly matching the spec 'revert if amount exceeds the contract balance'. Verify the boundary: withdraw(address(this).balance) should succeed, withdraw(address(this).balance + 1) should revert.

deposit() uses msg.value > 0 comparison, correctly matching the spec 'revert if msg.value is 0'. Verify the boundary: deposit with msg.value == 1 should succeed, deposit with msg.value == 0 should revert.

withdraw() should perform all state variable updates (e.g., decrementing balances) BEFORE the external .call{value: amount} at line 35 to follow the checks-effects-interactions pattern. Currently no state update occurs at all.
checklist.threat_model_filter: assumption: External calls via .call{} may trigger reentrant execution

checklist.property: During withdraw()'s external call at line 35, shared state variables (paused, balances, whitelist) can be read or modified by reentrant calls to deposit() or other functions. Verify that no cross-function reentrancy path leads to inconsistent state.
checklist.threat_model_filter: contract_owner is TRUSTED — cross-function reentrancy requires owner's address to be a contract; downgraded from critical to high

checklist.property: withdraw(amount) transfers exactly amount wei of ETH to msg.sender (the owner). Verify the owner's ETH balance increases by exactly amount after a successful withdrawal.
deposit() reverts when called with msg.value == 0, regardless of caller identity (owner or whitelisted).
withdraw(amount) reverts when amount > address(this).balance. Test with amount = address(this).balance + 1.
withdraw(0) edge case: verify behavior when amount is 0 and contract balance is 0. The require(amount <= balance) passes trivially, so withdraw(0) succeeds — confirm this is acceptable.
withdraw() function signature declares parameter 'amount' of type uint256, matching the interface specification.
addToWhitelist() function signature declares parameter 'addr' of type address, matching the interface specification.
removeFromWhitelist() function signature declares parameter 'addr' of type address, matching the interface specification.
